cross_connections.display_label: Junior partner at Isham's Chicago law firm from 1872; the firm bore his name for 116 years.
Attended the 1883 presidential big game hunt organized by Montana Governor J. Schuyler Crosby — father-in-law of Eunice Newton Foote's grandson.

narrative_blocks.body: Robert Todd Lincoln served as Secretary of War, Minister to Great Britain, and president of the Pullman Company. He joined Edward Swift Isham's Chicago firm as a junior partner in February 1872 — the same month his mother Mary Todd Lincoln was committed to an asylum. He spent his final years at Hildene, his Manchester, Vermont estate.

narrative_blocks.header: He joined Governor Crosby's presidential big game hunt
narrative_blocks.body: In 1883, when Robert Todd Lincoln was serving as Secretary of War under President Chester Arthur, he joined a presidential big game hunting expedition organized by Montana Governor John Schuyler Crosby. The party included President Arthur, Lincoln, Senator George Graham Vest, and other dignitaries — one of the largest organized hunts in American history. Crosby's father-in-law was the last patroon of Rensselaerwyck; his daughter Angelica later married John Brooks Henderson Jr., grandson of Eunice Newton Foote, the Hooker-descendant who published the first greenhouse effect paper.
